Title: ADP-glucose pyrophosphorylase genes, associated with kernel weight, underwent selection during wheat domestication and breeding

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Chinese Spring; Chinese wheat Mini Core Collection accessions; Chinese modern cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major grain storage carbohydrate contributing to kernel weight and yield
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ADP-glucose production step in cytosolic starch precursor synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of ADP-glucose from glucose-1-phosphate and ATP as the starch substrate-producing step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Considered a key enzyme in wheat endosperm starch synthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in developing grains/endosperm; transcript measured at 5, 10, 15, 20 and 25 DPA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trials in multiple years/environments (2002, 2005, 2006, 2010); developing grain stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Favoured haplotype Hap-I is associated with significantly higher thousand kernel weight than Hap-II
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaAGP-S1-7A was one of the only polymorphic AGPase homoeologs detected in the surveyed wheat collection. 2. A coding SNP at position 5090 caused an Ala-to-Ser substitution and was proposed to reduce enzyme activity in the nonfavoured haplotype. 3. Hap-I showed significantly higher thousand kernel weight than Hap-II in landraces and modern cultivars. 4. Favoured Hap-I frequency increased strongly during domestication and breeding, indicating positive selection. 5. The locus showed additive effects with TaAGP-L-1B and other starch-synthesis genes for improving kernel weight.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Haplotype sequencing; association analysis in MCC and MC populations; near-isogenic line comparison; CAPS marker validation; transcript analysis; evolutionary diversity/selection analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker-assisted selection target for increasing kernel weight; favoured haplotype can be pyramided with TaAGP-L-1B and sucrose synthase favoured haplotypes to improve yield
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TaAGP-S1-7A contains an alternative first exon producing plastid SSU in leaves; the reported focal allele is the cytosolic endosperm-expressed form on chromosome 7A. Selection mainly occurred during and after hexaploidization.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Chinese Spring; Chinese wheat Mini Core Collection accessions; Chinese modern cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major grain storage carbohydrate contributing to kernel weight and yield
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ADP-glucose production step in cytosolic starch precursor synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the large subunit of ADP-glucose pyrophosphorylase producing ADP-glucose for starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Considered a key enzyme in wheat endosperm starch synthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in developing grains/endosperm; transcript measured at 5, 10, 15, 20 and 25 DPA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trials in multiple years/environments (2002, 2005, 2006, 2010); developing grain stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Favoured haplotype Hap-I is associated with significantly higher thousand kernel weight than Hap-II; promoter SNP in Hap-II reduces expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaAGP-L-1B promoter and coding-region polymorphisms formed four haplotypes, with Hap-I associated with higher thousand kernel weight than Hap-II. 2. The distinct haplotype effect was attributed to a promoter SNP at position 122 located in an E2F-DP-binding motif. 3. Rice promoter-GUS assays showed Hap-I promoter drove significantly higher expression than Hap-II in developing seeds. 4. Strong positive selection for the favoured haplotype occurred during wheat domestication and breeding. 5. Favoured haplotypes showed additive effects with TaAGP-S1-7A and other starch synthesis genes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Haplotype sequencing; association analysis in MCC and MC populations; near-isogenic line comparison; promoter-GUS transgenic assay in rice; transcript analysis; CAPS marker validation; evolutionary diversity/selection analysis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native TaAGP-L-1B haplotype promoters; ~1.4 kb promoter fragments tested in transgenic rice
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value marker-assisted selection target; favoured promoter haplotype can be selected or engineered to elevate endosperm expression and increase kernel weight; useful in haplotype pyramiding with TaAGP-S1-7A and TaSus loci
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No amino acid changes distinguished the major functional haplotypes; the causal difference is linked mainly to promoter variation affecting transcript level. Strong selection occurred during hexaploidization, domestication and breeding.
